business_rationale: Under intense delivery deadlines, reducing approval delay by triggering a lower approval level can speed up procurement, thus helping meet customer delivery milestones while staying within allowed actions.
control_concern: Amount-based approval controls may be circumvented by strategically reporting amounts below threshold, risking insufficient oversight on large purchases critical to delivery.
expected_effect: Faster approval process due to manager-level approval rather than department_head, reducing lead time but potentially weakening control over large expenditure approvals.
proposed_behavior: Create a single high-value purchase request just below the department_head approval threshold by slightly underreporting the amount, aiming for manager-level approval to expedite processing under delivery deadline pressure.
review_evidence: Purchase request amounts clustered just below the 1,000,000 threshold within tight time windows before delivery deadlines.
Repeated patterns of purchase requests slightly under threshold by the same requester or department.
Comparison of approval levels triggered versus actual purchase need amounts and delivery urgency.
